name: Tuna Pockets
directions: In a medium bowl, stir together first three ingredients.
Mix with mayonnaise and orange juice.
Stuff pita with lettuce and tuna mix.

name: Tuna Salad or Sandwich Spread
directions: Put all ingredients into a bowl and mix well.
Serve on lettuce leaves for a luncheon or make sandwiches.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Crunchy Tuna Walnut Salad
directions: In bowl combine ingredients.
Cover, refrigerate at least 4 hours.
Serve in seeded sweet pepper, tomato or cucumber.

name: Chicadeli Tuna Sandwich
directions: In a large bowl combine all the ingredients.
Spread mixture into your favorite style bread.
Eat and have a BALL!

name: Tuna Salad
directions: Hard boil eggs.
Chop eggs.
Drain tuna.
In a small bowl mix tuna, eggs and mayo.

similar.name: Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make
Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Italian Style Tuna Spread
directions: Drain Tuna , sqeezing out water.
Mash the tuna in a small bowl and add the rest of the ingredients. Serve on bread or toast.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make
directions: Coarsely chop the hardboiled eggs and add to a bowl.
Flake in the tuna.
Add the pickle relish, and stir to combine.
Add the mayonnaise a little at a time, tasting as you go, until you get a consistency you like.  I like my tuna salad kind of creamy.
Season to taste with salt and pepper.
VARIATION:
Use canned salmon in place of the tuna.  Make sure to pick out all the pieces of bone and cartilage first.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Cheesy Tuna Burgers
directions: Combine tuna, mayonnaise, pickle, and onion; spread on hot dog buns.
Top each bun with 2 strips of cheese.  Broil until bubbly.

name: Tasty Tuna Salad
directions: Place tuna in a large bowl and flake well with a fork.
Add all remaining ingredients (start low with the mayo and work up to desired amount.
Mix well.
Chill for at least 2 hours for best flavor.

name: Tuna Wrap-Ups
directions: In a small bowl, mix tuna and mayonnaise together; season to taste with salt and pepper.
Arrange crescent rolls on an ungreased baking sheet.
Fold cheese slices diagonally and break into 2 pieces; place one piece on each crescent.
Spread 1 heaping tablespoon tuna mixture over cheese; roll up crescent roll-style.
Bake in a 375° oven for 12 minutes.

name: Tuna Salad
directions: Mix everything together and spread on sandwiches.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Fast Food Tuna Melt
directions: Toast bread.
Smear mayo on bread.
Put on tuna and slice cheese; put under broiler till melted or put in microwave about 30 seconds.
Add other slice bread or leave off--up to you.

name: Tangy Tarragon Tuna Salad
directions: Boil eggs until cooked all the way through, 5 minutes or so.
Drain water from tuna. Add to mixing bowl. Chop all ingredients to smaller than bite size. Mix with yogurt, sour cream (or mayonnaise), lemon juice and dill. Add salt and pepper to taste.
*I generally make this recipe by sight, so you may want to add the ingredients slowly until you feel it looks even.

name: Indigo's Ridiculously Good Tuna (Salad!)
directions: Mix all ingredients.
Chill about 20 minutes.  Or, if, like me, you can't wait for tuna delicousness, dig right in!

name: Tuna Melt on French Bread
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.  Mix together tuna, mayo, sour cream, and garlic powder.  Spread the mixture over the french bread that has been cut in half.  Put swiss cheese over tuna (enough to cover) and put the top of the loaf over tuna and cheese.  Wrap foil around the sandwich and bake for 30 minutes.  Cut into individual sandwiches and enjoy!

name: Herbed Tuna Sandwiches
directions: In a small bowl, combine the first seven ingredients.
Place four slices of toast on an ungreased baking sheet.
Top with the tuna mixture and sprinkle with cheese.
Broil 3-4" from the heat for 1-2 minutes or until cheese is melted.
Take out of the oven and top with remaining 4 slices of toast.

name: Spicy Tuna Melt
directions: Toast the bread.
Put the cheese on one slice (you may need to microwave for a couple seconds if it doesn't melt enough).
Mix together tuna and all other ingredients.
Throw in between bread slices.
Grab some chips and call it a day.

name: Tuna Salad
directions: In medium bowl, stir together tuna, mayonnaise and Parmesan cheese.
Season with curry powder, parsley, dill and garlic powder.
Mix well and serve with crackers or on a sandwich.

name: Easy Tuna Melt
directions: Mix together tuna and mayonnaise in a bowl until combined.
Spread on tortilla shell (like pizza sauce).
Top with parsley flakes and shredded cheese.
Broil for 5-10 minutes.
Cut like a pizza and enjoy!

name: Just Another Tuna Salad Sandwich
directions: Drain tuna and mix together with other ingredients.
Refrigerate until cold.
Serve on bread or pita with lettuce.

name: Easy Tuna Salad
directions: Chop boiled egg into small pieces.
Add drained can of tuna.
Add Light Miracle Whip and relish as desired.
Spread on crackers for appetizers or bread for a sandwich.

similar.name: Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make
Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Tuna Cheese Melts
directions: In a small bowl, combine the first five ingredients; stir in 2/3 cup cheese.  Spread over each muffin half; sprinkle with remaining cheese and paprika.
Place on a baking sheet.  Broil 3-4 inches from the heat for 2-4 minutes or until lightly browned and cheese is melted.

name: Hot Tuna Sandwich
directions: Wearing gloves, carefully remove seeds and chop the jalapeno pepper.
In a medium bowl, mix the cheese, tuna, jalapeno, lemon juice, mustard, salt, and cream cheese.
Scoop out the center of the hamburger buns.  Fill each bun with tuna mixture.
Wrap each sandwich in foil and heat at 425 degrees for 15-20 minutes.
Sandwiches can be frozen before heating.

name: Keep It Simple  Tuna Melt
directions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Mix the tuna, mayo and dill in a bowl.
Cut the Rolls so that they can be used as a sandwich.
Scoop some of the tuna mixture on one half and add a slice of cheddar on top of the tuna.
Place the both halves of the croissant on a baking dish for all the sandwiches.
This will toast the half without any tuna and cheese along with the half with the tuna and cheese.
Leave in the oven to melt/bake for 8 to 10 minutes.

name: My Take on Tuna Melts
directions: Prepare the tuna salad by combining tuna, mayonnaise, relish, celery salt, onion salt, and black pepper.  Taste and season again, if necessary.

Meanwhile, toast three slices of bread. (I used Mrs. Baird's Acti-Fiber bread and 2 slices of Honey Wheat bread for DH; really this is a go-with-your-favorite option.)  Toasting the bread is extremely important!  Don't skip the step.
Place toast on a cookie sheet.  Spread tuna salad on each slice.
Top each piece of toast with 2 slices roma tomato.  Grind fresh black pepper over each slice.
Cover with cheese.  DH used American singles, and I used Pepper Jack.  Both were great!

Set oven to Broil with rack around six inches from the burner.  Broil the sandwiches until cheese is "melty and bubbly."  Don't turn your back on the oven--the sandwiches go from melty and bubbly to black and burnt in a snap!
We served this with barbecue potato chips and yogurt for dessert.

name: Hold the Mayo Tuna Salad Sandwich
directions: In a bowl, mix together the tuna, vinegar, oil, lemon zest, lemon juice, capers, onions, olives, and parsley.
Season to taste with salt and pepper (better to refrigerate overnight to let flavors blend).
Make sandwich—place bread slices on work surface and drizzle lightly with olive oil.
Layer the tuna salad on 4 slices, followed by avocado and tomato.
Top with remaining bread, oil side down.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Tuna on Toast
directions: Sauté onions and pepper (if using) in butter until soft.  Remove from heat.
Whisk in flour to form smooth paste.  Gradually add milk stiring until smooth.
Place back on heat and bring to boil, stirring as you do.
Add cheese.  When melted, add tuna; stir until hot.
I like this over toast.

name: Veggie Tuna Salad
directions: Chop the onion, sprinkle with salt in a medium bowl and mix well.
Add the rest of the veggies and herbs to the bowl, mixing as you add each ingredient.
Add the vinegar and oil, mix well.
Add the tuna, mix well.

name: Apple and Cheddar Tuna Salad
directions: Combine first 8 ingredients in a bowl.
Stuff pita bread halves with tuna mixture and enjoy!

name: Nutty Tuna Salad
directions: mix well.
put into sandwiches or on a lettuce leaf for garnish.

name: Salsa Tuna Melts
directions: Toast your bread until it is light golden brown.  (If it gets too dark during this step, the edges will burn when you put it in the oven/toaster oven later.).
Drain excess liquid from the salsa if it is very juicy.  Otherwise, the tuna mixture will be too runny, and the toast will become soggy.
While the bread is toasting, mix the tuna, mayonnaise and salsa in a small bowl.
Spread some of the tuna mixture on each piece of toast.
Place slices on a foil-lined pan in the toaster oven and heat until tuna mixture is warmed through, about 3-5 minutes.
Top each piece with a slice of cheese, and put back in oven until cheese is melted.
Enjoy!

name: Lemony Tuna Patties
directions: Combine all ingredients except 1/2 cup cracker crumbs; mix well.
Shape into 12 patties.
Coat with cracker crumbs.
Brown on both sides in margarine in a large skillet at medium heat.
Serve with tater sauce or lemon.
May heat in the microwave on high for 3 - 5 minutes.

name: Tuna Burger
directions: Drain tuna. Beat egg, add tuna. Sprinkle on the spices. Mix with fork until well blended. Add bread crumbs until mixture is damp, but not wet. Shape into patties. Cook in skillet with oil, I use coconut, until brown on both sides.
Top with sharp cheddar or Swiss cheese, place under broiler either of the oven or toaster oven until cheese is bubbly.
Serve with mayo, lettuce and fresh tomato on bread or a bun.

name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)
directions: Combine tuna, eggs, capers, pepper, onion and tarragon gently in a bowl.
Whisk together vinegar, oil, salt and pepper in another small bowl.
Fold the dressing into the tuna mixture. Taste for seasoning, and add more salt or pepper as needed.
Chill for 1 hour for flavors to combine, then serve.

similar.name: Hold the Mayo Tuna Salad Sandwich
Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make

name: Healthier Tuna Salad
directions: Drain tuna.
Mix all in bowl.
Serve on toast or crackers.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)

name: Spicy Tuna Waldorf Salad
directions: Drain the tuna and flake with a fork. Place into a mixing bowl.
Combine the other ingredients and add to the bowl with the tuna.
Add the mayo and stir gently to combine.
Serve with your favorite toasted bread.

name: Janet's Tuna Salad
ingredients: solid white tuna packed in water
directions: Drain and rinse the tuna and mix together. Mash to the texture you like.
Chop the celery, carrot and onion to 1/4 cup each.
Chop the dill pickles to 1-2 tablespoons.
Mix all ingredients together.
Salt and pepper to taste.

similar.name: Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make

name: Tuna, Sweetcorn, and Pepper Melt
directions: Preheat the sandwich toaster to its highest setting.
Deseed the pepper and finely dice. Mix together with the tuna, mayonnaise, and sweetcorn, and season with a little black pepper.
Spread the tuna mixture over one slice of bread, place the cheese slices on top, and cover the sandwich with the second slice of bread.
Place the sandwich in the toaster, slowly close the lid, and cook for 3 1/2 minutes until crisp. Serve immediately.

name: Tuna and White Bean Salad
directions: Drain tuna, reserving 1 tablespoon oil. In small bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar and mustard.
In medium bowl, combine remaining ingredients.  Toss with dressing to coat.

name: Tuna and White Bean Salad
directions: Drain tuna, reserving 1 tablespoon oil. In small bowl, whisk together oil, vinegar and mustard.
In medium bowl, combine remaining ingredients.  Toss with dressing to coat.

name: Vintage Tuna Salad
directions: In medium bowl, combine all ingredients.

similar.name: Tuna Salad With a Greek Accent

name: Tuna Salad With a Greek Accent
directions: In a small bowl, combine all ingredients with a fork.

name: Mediterranean Tuna Salad
directions: In a bowl, combine all ingredients with a fork.

similar.name: Completely Different Tuna & Egg Salad (No Mayo)
Tuna Salad With a Greek Accent

name: Dilly Tuna Salad
directions: Combine all ingredients in a medium bowl and chill overnight.
Chill overnight.

similar.name: Tuna Salad #1 : Like Mom Used to Make

name: Balsamic Tuna Salad
directions: Drain the can of tuna and empty into a bowl.
Finely chop the green onions and pour in the bowl with the tuna.
Do the same with the cranberries.
Pour in the vinegar and oil.
Sprinkle enough salt and pepper to taste.
Mix it up.
Put it on some bread, a salad, anything, or nothing (eat it just like it is).
